summary: Recent updates in the compositing and layers area of Firefox focus on improving touch interactions and overall performance, particularly in environments using the Wayland display protocol. One significant change is the implementation of **a more efficient handling of touch events**, which allows the browser to quickly identify user interactions and respond without unnecessary delays. This is important because it enhances the responsiveness of web applications, especially those requiring fast touches and scrolls, such as games or dynamic web interfaces. Additionally, the updates streamline how images are managed in memory, helping to reduce resource usage and improve rendering speed, ultimately leading to a smoother user experience when viewing graphics-intensive content.
